Ukraine Conflict News





26 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • US launches airstrikes on Nigeria, citing unsubstantiated claims of anti-Christian violence Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- The United States has launched airstrikes in northwest Nigeria, with President Donald Trump casting the attack as a response to alleged anti-Christian violence—an assertion Nigerian authorities and analysts have long rejected as a misleading pretext for military action.
  • US Carried Out 'Deadly Strike' on ISIS Targets in Northwest Nigeria — Trump Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump said the United States launched a "powerful and deadly strike" against what he described as ISIS* targets in northwest Nigeria.
  • U.S. military strikes IS in northwest Nigeria, Trump says People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that the U.S. military launched a "powerful and deadly" strike against the Islamic State (IS) in northwestern Nigeria.

  • Interception of Venezuelan Tankers by US Could Pose Security Risks - Chinese Ministry Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- China opposes the blockade of Venezuelan oil and the seizure of tankers, as these actions could pose security risks, Chinese Commerce Ministry spokesperson He Yongqian said.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Meeting on the main parameters of the State Armament Programme for 2027-2036 President of Russia 26 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin chaired a meeting to discuss the key parameters of the draft State Armament Programme for 2027-2036 and the state programme to develop defence manufacturing.
  • Production of Arms in High Demand in Russia Increased Exponentially Since 2022 - Putin Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- The production of weapons in high demand in Russia during the special military operation has increased exponentially since 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Friday.
  • Northern Fleet's major ASW ship Vice Admiral Kulakov completes long-range mission and returns to base Russia MoD 26 Dec 2025 -- The Northern Fleet's major ASW ship Vice Admiral Kulakov completed a long-range mission and returns to the base.
  • Russia to Repurpose Burevestnik, Poseidon Materials for Civilian Use - Official Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- Russia will repurpose advanced materials and alloys used in the Burevestnik nuclear-powered cruise missile and Poseidon underwater drone for civilian use, including in the energy and space industries, Russian Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov said.
  • Oreshnik Deployed in Belarus Can Carry Nuclear, Conventional Warheads Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- The Oreshnik missile system deployed in Belarus can carry both nuclear and conventional warheads, Belarusian Defense Minister Viktor Khrenin said on Friday.

  • Nine IRIS-T systems guarding Ukraine's skies: what is known about these modern air defense systems Ukraine MOD 26 Dec 2025
  • Defense manufacturers to access leasing at 5% annually as the state introduces a preferential support mechanism Ukraine MOD 26 Dec 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal: banking sector unites to provide ₴21.5 billion in financing for the defense industry Ukraine MOD 26 Dec 2025
  • Government introduces long-term contracts for production under the 'Weapons of Victory' program Ukraine MOD 26 Dec 2025

  • Two ships of 48th Chinese naval escort taskforce make technical stop in Kenya PRC MND 26 Dec 2025 -- The guided-missile destroyer Tangshan (Hull 122) and the comprehensive supply ship Taihu (Hull 889) attached to the 48th Chinese naval escort taskforce arrived at the port of Mombasa in Kenya on the morning of December 25, 2025, for a four-day technical stop.

  • Decision on Taking Countermeasures Against U.S. Military-Related Companies and Senior Executives PRC MofA 26 Dec 2025 -- The United States recently announced its large-scale arms sales to China's Taiwan Region. This seriously violates the one-China principle and the three China-U.S. Joint Communiqués, interferes in China's internal affairs, and undermines China's sovereignty and territorial integrity.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Countermeasures Against U.S. Arms Sales to China's Taiwan Region PRC MofA 26 Dec 2025
  • China imposes countermeasures against 20 US companies, 10 senior executives in response to US arms sales to Taiwan Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- In response to the latest US announcement of large-scale arms sales to China's Taiwan region, China has decided to take countermeasures in accordance with the Anti-Foreign Sanctions Law against 20 US defense-related companies and 10 senior executives who have engaged in arming Taiwan in recent years, the Chinese Foreign Ministry announced on Friday.
  • China sanctions 20 US firms, executives over massive arms sales to Taiwan Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Beijing has imposed sanctions on 20 US defense-related companies and 10 executives, following Washington's announcement of large-scale arms sales to Taiwan.
  • China strongly urges U.S. not to implement negative China-related provisions in defense policy bill People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- China strongly urges the United States to abandon zero-sum mindset and ideological bias, and refrain from implementing the negative China-related provisions in its defense policy bill, a spokesperson for the Foreign Affairs Committee of the National People's Congress said on Thursday, in response to the U.S. side recently signing into law the "National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026."

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Gives Field Guidance at Major Munitions Industry Enterprises KCNA 26 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, visited major munitions industry enterprises to acquaint himself with the missile and shell production in the fourth quarter of this year.
  • North Korea accelerates nuclear navy, slams US-South Korea submarine deal an 'offensive act' Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong Un called a plan by South Korea to develop nuclear-powered submarines with the US an "offensive act" while inspecting his country's first nearly completed nuclear-powered submarine,

  • Why the Republic of Korea needs nuclear-powered submarines KOREA.net 26 Dec 2025

  • Japan-U.S. Joint Committee Statement on Unilateral Patrol by the U.S. Military Police Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 26 Dec 2025
  • Japan's cabinet approves record defense budget amid tensions with China Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Japan's Cabinet has approved a record defense budget of 9.04 trillion yen ($58 billion) for fiscal 2026 as tensions with China continue to spiral.
  • China urges Japan to avoid compounding wrongdoing, slams its defense budget hike Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- China urged Japan on Friday to face squarely and deeply reflect on Japan's history of aggression, take concrete actions to show remorse for the crimes committed by Japanese militarists and respect to the victims, and not further compound the wrongdoing while slamming Tokyo's plan to significantly increase its defense budget as further exposing the sinister intent of Japanese right-wing forces to push for remilitarization and attempt a revival of militarism.
  • Chinese FM slams Japan's defense budget hike plan as 'exposing Japanese right-wing forces' sinister intent to push for remilitarization, militarism revival' Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- A Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson on Friday slammed Japan's plan to significantly increase its defense budget as further exposing its sinister intent of Japanese right-wing forces to push for remilitarization and attempt a revival of militarism.
  • Chinese FM voices serious concern, 'firmly opposes Japan's dangerous moves in the cyber domain' Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- Commenting on the report that Japanese Cabinet recently approved a new "Cybersecurity Strategy," authorizing the police and self-defense forces to take proactive countermeasures in the event of cyberattacks, and Japan also passed the so-called active cyber defense in May this year, which allows the Japanese government to preemptively intrude into or destroy the networks and equipment of so-called attackers, Lin Jian, a spokesperson from China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, said on Friday that Japanese Cabinet's move to explicitly authorize "proactive cyber counterattacks" marks a major policy shift from cyber defense to cyber offense, and represents yet another example of Japan — as a defeated country in World War II — attempting to break away from the post-war international order.

  • Turkey seeking to deploy radars in Syria to dismay of Israel: Reports Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Turkey has reportedly been trying to install radar systems on Syrian soil in recent weeks, much to the dismay of the Israeli regime.

  • Reinforcing PH Navy's missile capabilities amid WPS tensions Philippine News Agency 26 Dec 2025 -- With the commissioning of the two Miguel Malvar-class guided-missile frigates this year, the Philippine Navy (PN) has effectively reinforced its missile capability — a significant upgrade in the face of lingering tensions in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).
  • AFP sees more robust modernization in 2026 Philippine News Agency 26 Dec 2025 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) is expecting a more robust modernization in 2026.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • PM Netanyahu and his wife send condolences to the families of those who were murdered in the combined terror attack in the North Israel - Prime Minister 26 Dec 2025

  • Critical Gaza hospital runs out of fuel, suspends most services Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- A major hospital in the central part of the besieged Gaza Strip has been forced to suspend most of its medical services after running out of fuel to power its generators.

  • Israeli airstrikes hit eastern, southern Lebanon in fresh ceasefire breach Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Israeli warplanes launched fresh airstrikes across eastern and southern Lebanon on Friday, extending a pattern of ceasefire violations that has continued for months.

  • UN peacekeeper injured in gunfire near 'Blue Line' in south Lebanon UN News 26 Dec 2025 -- One United Nations peacekeeper was injured on Friday after heavy machine gunfire from Israel Defense Forces (IDF) positions south of the Blue Line, following a nearby grenade explosion, impacted close to a UN patrol in southern Lebanon.
  • UNRWA Humanitarian Appeal 2026: Syria, Lebanon and Jordan UNRWA 26 Dec 2025

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Mali, Nigeria UNOCHA 26 Dec 2025

  • Yemen says ready for next inevitable war, urges Muslims to be vigilant Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Yemen's Ansarullah leader says the country is preparing day and night for the next inevitable round of war with the United States and Israel, vowing that Yemeni resistance will not be halted or disarmed.
  • U.S. Welcomes Saudi and Emirati Leadership Supporting Yemen's Sovereignty and Regional Security US Dept. of State 26 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's comment on the escalation of tensions in southern Yemen Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Dec 2025
  • Pakistan expresses support for efforts for peace and stability in Yemen Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Dec 2025 -- Pakistan is closely following the recent developments in Yemen.

  • On 25 December 2025, security forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Kohlu District of Balochistan, on reported presence of terrorists belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Hindustan. ISPR Pakistan 26 Dec 2025

  • OIC General Secretariat Condemns the Terrorist Attack Targeting the Imam Ali Bin Abi Talib Mosque in Homs, Syria Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Dec 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned the terrorist attack that targeted the Imam Ali Bin Abi Talib Mosque in Homs, Syria, during Friday prayers, resulting in the death and injury of a number of innocent people
  • Iran condemns terrorist attack on Imam Ali Mosque in Syria IRNA 26 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has strongly condemned the terrorist attack on a mosque in Syria.
  • Iran strongly condemns deadly terrorist attack targeting worshippers in Syria's Homs Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- The Foreign Ministry has strongly condemned a terrorist attack on worshippers at the Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS) Mosque in Syria's Homs Province that left many dead and injured during Friday prayers.
  • Syria: Guterres deplores deadly mosque blast in Homs UN News 26 Dec 2025 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres "unequivocally condemns" the deadly terrorist attack on a mosque in Homs, Syria during Friday prayers, his spokesperson said in a statement
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on attack on mosque in Syria UN Secretary-General 26 Dec 2025
  • At least 8 killed in terrorist attack on mosque during Friday prayers in Syria's Homs Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- A terrorist attack struck the Imam Ali Mosque in the Wadi al-Dhahab neighborhood of Homs on Friday, killing and wounding worshippers during weekly prayers, according to Syrian media and regional networks.

  • Top commander among 6 Naxals killed in India's Odisha gunfight People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- A top commander was among the six Naxals killed in a gunfight with police in the eastern Indian state of Odisha, officials said Thursday.

  • FM Sa'ar speaks with President of Somaliland Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi Israel MFA 26 Dec 2025 -- FM Sa'ar: "We will work together to promote the relations between our countries and nations, regional stability and economic prosperity. I have instructed my ministry to act immediately to institutionalize ties between the two countries across a wide range of fields."
  • Israel recognizes the Republic of Somaliland as an independent and sovereign state Israel MFA 26 Dec 2025 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu, Foreign Minister Sa'ar, and the President of the Republic of Somaliland signed a joint and mutual declaration. This declaration is in the spirit of the Abraham Accords, signed at the initiative of President Trump.
  • PM Netanyahu announced the official recognition of the Republic of Somaliland as an independent and sovereign state Israel - Prime Minister 26 Dec 2025
  • Israel recognizes Somaliland as state, drawing international condemnation Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- The Israeli regime has announced its recognition of the self-declared Republic of Somaliland as a sovereign state, a move swiftly condemned by Somalia and several regional powers who warn it violates international law and threatens regional stability.
  • Organization of Islamic Cooperation Strongly Condemns Israel's Recognition of the "Somaliland" Region Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Dec 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation expressed its strong condemnation and categorical rejection of Israel's recognition, as the occupying power, of the "Somaliland" region as an independent state, considering it a violation of the sovereignty of the Federal Republic of Somalia, its national unity, and its territorial integrity

  • UN renews ceasefire push in Sudan UN News 26 Dec 2025 -- The UN has urged Sudan's warring parties to pursue compromise and an immediate ceasefire, even as drone attacks, displacement and the killing of peacekeepers underscore the growing risks to civilians and humanitarians.
  • Sudan civil war: Health system 'on the verge of collapse' UN News 26 Dec 2025 -- The war in Sudan has been tearing the country apart for almost 1,000 days, putting the country's health system under intolerable pressure. The World Health Organization (WHO) is reporting widespread disease outbreaks, severe shortages, malnutrition and rising deaths.

News Reports

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 26 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held, via video conference, a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • Key foreign policy outcomes of 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to a media question regarding the practice of unilateral coercive measures Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Dec 2025
  • Moscow says West uses sanctions to cling to fading dominance Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Russia has said that the collective West uses its sanctions against other countries to cling to its fading dominance.
  • Unfriendly Countries Admit 'Strategic Defeat' on Russia Impossible - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 26 Dec 2025 -- Unfriendly countries were forced to acknowledge the impossibility of inflicting a "strategic defeat" on Russia and switched to calling for an immediate ceasefire in Ukraine, the Russian Foreign Ministry said on Friday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on December 26, 2025 PRC MofA 26 Dec 2025
  • China firmly opposes U.S. additional tariff on semiconductors from China People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- China disagrees with the so-called conclusions of the U.S. Section 301 investigation and firmly opposes the imposition of additional Section 301 tariffs on Chinese semiconductor products, a spokesperson for the Ministry of Commerce said on Thursday.
  • MSS warns of hostile foreign force using deepfakes to spread fake videos in China to create panic Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) warned on Friday that a foreign anti-China hostile force used deepfake technology to produce fabricated videos and attempted to disseminate them inside China to mislead public opinion and create panic, posing a threat to China's national security.
  • China's Long March-8A rocket launches internet satellites Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- China launched a Long March-8A carrier rocket on Friday in the southern island province of Hainan, sending a group of internet satellites into space.
  • 2025 Yearender: China space delivers another year of exciting achievements, marveling the world Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- "It is like standing in Beijing and throwing a basketball into a fixed hoop in Shanghai; the level of precision required is similarly demanding," Wei Yuanming of China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation (CASC) told the Global Times when he was illustrating the exciting and highly challenging Tianwen-2 mission in Xichang, Southwest China's Sichuan Province, in May, ahead of the launch of the first asteroid probe and sampling mission.

  • Legislature passes motion to start impeachment proceedings against Lai CNA 26 Dec 2025 -- The Legislative Yuan on Friday approved a motion to initiate impeachment proceedings against President Lai Ching-te (賴清德), arguing that he has undermined Taiwan's constitutional order and democracy.
  • Cabinet aims to tighten rules on lawmaker, civil servant China visits CNA 26 Dec 2025 -- The Cabinet on Friday approved amendments that would expand the range of individuals who would be required to get government approval before visiting China to lawmakers and all civil servants.
  • Taiwan lawmakers vote to pass impeachment proposal against Lai ching-te, announce full timetable Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- With 60 votes in favor and 51 against, the legislative body in China's Taiwan region approved an impeachment proposal from opposition parties against regional leader Lai Ching-te on Friday. Following the formal initiation of the case against Lai, opposition parties in Taiwan also announced their planned timetable for the impeachment proceedings on the same day, according to Taiwan-based media reports.
  • Taiwan question at the very core of China's core interests, the first red line must not be crossed in China-US relations: FM on countermeasures against US arms sales to China's Taiwan Global Times 26 Dec 2025
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 26 Dec 2025
  • Fujian coast guard conducts routine patrol in waters near Kinmen Global Times 26 Dec 2025 -- The Fujian coast guard has recently conducted law enforcement patrols in waters near Kinmen in accordance with the law, a spokesperson for a regional bureau of the China Coast Guard said on Thursday.

  • Gharibabadi: Enemy's strikes on civilians during war proved nuclear issue was just an excuse IRNA 26 Dec 2025 -- Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs Kazem Gharibabadi has emphasized that the enemy's attacks that struck civilians and residential areas during the illegal and unprovoked Israeli-American war on the Islamic Republic in June, demonstrated that the nuclear issue was merely a pretext for targeting the country.
  • Iran, Russia share stance against US dominance, Araghchi says IRNA 26 Dec 2025 -- Iran and Russia hold aligned positions in opposing the United States' "hegemonic" policies, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has said during a recent visit to Russia.
  • Iran FM: US pursuing 'law of jungle' over diplomacy Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has stated that the United States pursues "the law of jungle" over diplomacy, resorting to force and sanctions to advance its interests.
  • Iran to confiscate $5mn seized tanker, fuel cargo in favor of government: Official Press TV 26 Dec 2025 -- The chief justice of Iran's Hormozgan Province says a recently seized tanker and its fuel cargo worth more than five million dollars will be transferred to government ownership.
  • Iran's Missiles In Focus As Netanyahu Heads To Washington RFE/RL 26 Dec 2025

  • Development cooperation agreement marks new phase in UN-Iraq partnership UN News 26 Dec 2025 -- Better access to education, the protection of the environment and good governance are some of the areas in which the United Nations helps countries improve.

  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (December 26, 2025) India MEA 26 Dec 2025

  • Somalia's capital kicks off first direct elections in over five decades People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- More than 500,000 voters in Somalia's capital, Mogadishu, cast ballots on Thursday in municipal elections widely seen as a key step toward the country's first direct national elections in more than five decades, scheduled for 2026.

  • Bangladesh Nationalist Party's acting chairperson returns home after 17 years in exile People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP)'s acting chairperson Tarique Rahman returned to the country Thursday after spending 17 years in exile in Britain.

  • China urges Japan to reflect on history of aggression People's Daily 26 Dec 2025 -- China urges Japan to face up to and do soul-searching on its history of aggression, and show repentance for militaristic crimes and respect for the victims through concrete actions, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said on Friday.

  • PM call with President Macron of France and Chancellor Merz of Germany: 26 December 2025 Gov.UK 26 Dec 2025

  • Türk calls on Guinea authorities to end pre-election intimidation and violations OHCHR 26 Dec 2025

  • Guinea Bissau: Authorities must end arbitrary detentions and all forms of intimidation OHCHR 26 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Honduras President-Elect Asfura US Dept. of State 26 Dec 2025
